title: Orrery
source: location-framing.md → Volume: Orrery (anchor orrery); component detail + base mechanism probed in-game via tools/play.cjs
frame: +X east, +Z south, +Y up. Wide TALL six-sided chamber. The brass disk is a free-spinning PERFECT CIRCLE on a tilted axis (~4-5deg back from vertical), LIFTED clear of the floor so its whole rim reads as a turning wheel; a separate GEAR DRIVE sits below it, cogs meshing its lower rim (the gears are NOT part of the disk). The two sub-wheels stand PROUD of the disk's face on spindles, turning freely and carrying their orbs. Alcove cameras (N/S) glimpse the same machine through their archways.

parts.name: ceiling
parts.detail: high roof; the vault above the machine is lost in shadow

parts.name: orrery disk
parts.detail: the great brass disk: a free-spinning PERFECT CIRCLE on a tilted axis, lifted clear of the floor so its full rim shows; engraved with arcs/lines; turns about the fixed central hub globe

parts.name: blue-glass globe (centre/hub, fixed)
parts.detail: large fixed blue-glass globe at the disk's hub (brown/white island-map pattern); the disk rotates around it

parts.name: silver wheel spindle
parts.detail: short spindle standing the silver wheel proud of the disk face so it turns freely in front of it

parts.name: silver wheel (upper side)
parts.detail: silver sub-wheel standing proud of the disk face; turns freely, carrying the tan globe around the violet hub

parts.name: violet globe (silver wheel hub)
parts.detail: violet globe at the silver wheel's CENTRE — the tan globe orbits around THIS one

parts.name: silver wheel arm (hub to rim)
parts.detail: radial arm carrying the tan globe around the violet hub — angled so the orbit reads from the front

parts.name: tan globe (silver wheel rim)
parts.detail: tan globe out on the silver wheel's arm, freely circling the violet hub; ringed with frosted white glass (blue mask caught in the rings); swings to the floor at its nadir

parts.name: copper wheel spindle
parts.detail: short spindle standing the copper wheel proud of the disk face

parts.name: copper wheel (lower side)
parts.detail: copper sub-wheel proud of the disk face; turns freely, carrying the green globe around the red hub

parts.name: red globe (copper wheel hub)
parts.detail: red globe at the copper wheel's CENTRE — the green globe orbits around THIS one

parts.name: copper wheel arm (hub to rim)
parts.detail: radial arm carrying the green globe around the red hub

parts.name: green globe (copper wheel rim)
parts.detail: small pocked green globe out on the copper wheel's arm, freely circling the red hub

parts.name: rear axle pylon
parts.detail: support pylon behind the disk carrying the tilted axle to the hub

parts.name: axle shaft to hub
parts.detail: tilted axle from the rear pylon into the disk's central hub

parts.name: gear drive base
parts.detail: separate gear-drive housing BELOW the lifted disk — the disk's lower rim turns just above it; render rich gears/shafts here

parts.name: drive cog south (meshes rim)
parts.detail: large brass cog whose teeth mesh the disk's lower rim, driving its rotation

parts.name: drive cog north (meshes rim)
parts.detail: large brass cog meshing the disk's lower rim on the other side

parts.name: drive cog small
parts.detail: smaller idler cog in the drive train

parts.name: dangling grey strip
parts.detail: thin grey RIBBON of soft cloth-like material hanging limp from the dark vault over the disk's upper edge — out of reach; a soft dangling strip, not a rigid pole

parts.name: south alcove floor
parts.detail: small curved alcove off the south side

parts.name: south alcove ladder rail W
parts.detail: iron ladder up a shaft (→ Starry Dome North)

parts.name: north alcove floor
parts.detail: small curved alcove off the north side

parts.name: north alcove stairs
parts.detail: archway onto a flight of ascending stairs (→ Curving Hall West End)

notes.orrery: DIM, deeply shadowed chamber — faint light, brass catching low warm highlights, high vault lost in blackness. The great disk is a FREE-SPINNING PERFECT CIRCLE turning on its central hub, lifted clear of the floor; caught MID-ROTATION (it is in motion, not a fixed dial/plaque/astrolabe). A separate engine of GEARS and shafts sits BELOW it, cogs meshing its lower rim and driving it — render the gearwork richly, but keep the disk a clean unbroken circle, distinct from the gears. The two sub-wheels stand PROUD of the disk face and turn FREELY on spindles, each carrying its globe around a hub (tan around violet; green around red); they are not flat inlays. Blue hub globe fixed at centre. Eye-level, pressed back against the entry (east) wall, the disk filling the frame.
notes.south-alcove: stand in the small alcove looking through the archway at the SAME machine turning in the chamber; iron ladder up a shaft beside you.
notes.north-alcove: stand in the alcove; archway onto ascending stairs; the same machine glimpsed turning through the opening.
